title: Rei Ayanami in The Economist
wikitext:
[]: Rei Ayanami in The Economist ...             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:20 am ... in an article on child pornography in Japan. http://www.economist.com/world/asia/displaystory.cfm?story_id=15731382 Comments on this article that mention Evangelion: "What on earth does a frame from 'Evangelion' have to do with your subject? Did you just grab the first cartoon-related picture you could find? I don't want to be rude, but it doesn't make you look very professional." "Now, you are using the picture of "Evangelion" in an article describing child pornography. I do not know how you can connect the dots between the two." "Regarding the relevance of a frame from the Evangelion franchise: Actually, and controversially, there were pornographic images of Rei and other female characters released by the company owning the rights to the series." "Unfortunately, the Economist has made a quite good choice this time, as the main characters in Evangelion are between 16-18 years old (They go to Senior High School together), and the last feature-length episode of the original series opened with an explicitly sexual scene." "Yes the first episode of Evangelion did have an overtly sexual theme with 16 - 18 year old high schoolers but this in itself uncovers several questions." -------------------------------------------------------------- Now, this article speaks volumes to me. The Economist is not written by idiots (although they may be fucking toffs) and is not targeted at idiots, but even they see fit to use a picture of Rei in the aforementioned context, and influence people's perception of Evangelion in a negative way (in the sense of pulling attention away from its central psychological themes). Regardless of whether this is justified or not, it shows that outside anime fans, the reputation of Evangelion is not good, quite possibly a PR disaster. If The Economist does this, then imagine what thicker people can come up with. The comment about the Evangelion franchise shows how the shit merchandise produced can negatively influence people's perception directly (person making the comment) and indirectly (through person reading the comment). This article calls attention to the importance of outreach to non-anime fans in order to stop Evangelion's rep sliding down the toilet and becoming totally irrelevant.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:31 am It's a shame the image of Rei is all they used of her, because Anno's statements about Japan's fascination with her as a sex icon in The Atlantic's article "Let's Die Together" are relevant and fascinating.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 17, 2010 6:37 pm They should have used a screenshot of Rei changing into her plugsuit. Or maybe this (without the thought bubble):

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Sat Apr 17, 2010 6:39 pm I think you're being a bit paranoid. Evangelion isn't mainstream media, it's reputation isn't bad outside anime circles, it's non-existent. Either way, it's not like Eva is entirely irrelevant in regards to the subject but since the article itself does not actually make any reference to it whatsoever I kinda suspect that image was simply chosen at random. As for those comments. Reading through them myself, the vibe I'm getting is that a lot of the commenters are in fact people from the anime community. And a few comments are not gonna be enough to catch the public eye and ignite some sort of smear campaign aimed at Evangelion. I mean, that would be ridiculous. How did that voting go anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 17, 2010 7:18 pm Meh. The writer of this article is just trying to put a specific and familiar face on something he's generalizing about. And since the writer really couldn't, in good conscience, actually post a child-porn manga image (or a non-explicit image from one, therefore "endorsing" or a least "advertising" it's existence), he merely grabed what the SafeSearch allowed him to grab. (Friggin' Naruto shows up in Google's SafeSearch of "Child Porn Anime".) The article itself remains thoroughly vague in defining what the writer believe to be "Child Pornography". Aside from the description of an Eva unrelated hentai manga, (that he somehow managed to read...) the writer fails to provide an actually definition of what he believes to be pornoography, much less child porn. If the writer includes images of Rei from Evangelion, then baby cupids may also be in his definition of kiddie porn. OH, THE SEXUAL EXPLOITATION OF THE MINOR DEPICTED ABOVE! And to top that, the wings make it a furry kiddie porn. How sick is that? To be honest, not that sick at all; and not too far removed from the "sexual" Rei images, either. This is an issue that has always depended somewhat on the conscience of the individual. Yes, there are some acts that are clearly sexual exploitation of minors. But there are a lot of cases where shots of nude children are only arousing to those who don't go out much. (What is one to do with all of the topless images of Astro-Girl in the original 60's Astro-Boy anime series?) Simply because a child is nude, doesn't mean it's sexually exploitable. If that were the case, bathing children would be outlawed as well. ("Show me on the doll where your parents cleaned you.") Even in the example given of End of Evangelion , one can't really say that the opening scene was glorifying the sexual content. As a matter of fact, it seemed more like it was frowning upon it. And if a scene seems to frown upon sexual exploitation among minors, then it can't really be seen as sexually exploiting minors.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:01 pm in the whole scheme of the kiddie porn/naked children in entertainment controversy, i'm waiting for the day that naked children and babies in older movies (such as Richard Donner's Superman & the kid taking a bath in that one movie with Michael J. Fox & John Jett) are labeled "kiddie porn" trust me, I have this funny feeling its gonna happen

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 18, 2010 2:23 am That picture of Rei looking vaguely shocked came over more as a "They're looking at porn of us" image to me. But let's consider -- would it have been better or worse overall had they used Rin from Kodomo no Jikan instead?

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Sun Apr 18, 2010 2:32 pm ^ I didn't even who what that was until I looked it up, and the answer is better.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Mon Apr 19, 2010 3:45 am planet news wrote: I didn't even who what that was

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Thu Apr 22, 2010 8:28 am It's probably because Rei is such a generally recognized figure in anime that they decided to use her. Whether that was to criticize Evangelion itself is up to opinion. But someone needs to do the job. Besides, there's a ton of Eva doujins out there so the series certainly isn't innocent here.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Apr 22, 2010 12:29 pm ^ Yeah, I stay away from that stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Thu Apr 22, 2010 5:34 pm For what it's worth, Rei is a pretty good icon for how Japanese culture sexualizes and fetishizes adolescents. I mean, she's hot yeah, but she is 14-15, if even that old, depending on how long it takes to grow a new Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Apr 23, 2010 1:56 am SenorSquiid wrote: but she is 10-11, if even that old

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Apr 23, 2010 10:51 am My God, this is the most outrageous EVA news I have ever heard. WE MUST BAN THOSE THAT SHOW OFF BAD IMAGES OF POPULAR CHARACTERS! IT'S DISGUSTING AND UNETHICAL! (OK, sure it was already done in EoE, BUT STILL!)

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Fri Apr 23, 2010 12:49 pm Rei seems to be thinking, "BITCH SAY WHAT?" That being said, I hate it when articles generalize anime when making commentary on hentai/lolicon. That being said, they just used Rei because she's recognizable. I still disagree with this article. So what they make porn of anime children. Big freaking deal. It's not like any children were harmed in the making of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Apr 23, 2010 8:33 pm Reichu wrote: Or maybe this (without the thought bubble):

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 24, 2010 3:20 am Merridian wrote: let's forget the fact that this "14-year old Rei body" is so disproportionate to typical real 14 year old that it's ridiculous to even make the connection.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat Apr 24, 2010 9:36 pm I didn't realize that it was really her, lol. I guess the author didn't feel like doing that much research, but I don't think it was malicious either. Nothing is going to happen.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Sun Apr 25, 2010 1:03 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: WHERE DID WE GET OFF TAKING ABOUT ADOLESCENT BOOBS?

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sun Apr 25, 2010 7:04 am ^ For whatever reason, I Lol'd. You're officially off my shit list now that I realized that you have a sense of humor.
